query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Discursiveness', 'Passing aimlessly from one subject to another, digressive', 'The essay highlights the discursiveness of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Discussion', 'The action or process of talking about something', 'Her discussion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disdain', 'The feeling that someone is unworthy of respect', 'Her disdain became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disencumber', 'To remove a burden', 'Over time, the community learned to disencumber its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disenfranchise', 'To deprive someone of a right of citizenship', 'The author hoped the new policy would disenfranchise tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disengage', 'To release or loosen from something that binds, unfasten', 'Critics argued that the plan might disengage the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disentangle', 'To free something from entanglement, to extricate or unknot', 'Critics argued that the plan might disentangle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disfavor', 'Unfavorable regard, displeasure, disesteem, dislike', 'Her disfavor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disfigure', 'Change the appearance of something/someone to the negative', 'Critics argued that the plan might disfigure the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disgruntle', 'To put into a bad mood or into bad humour', 'Critics argued that the plan might disgruntle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dishabille', 'Extreme casual or disorderly dress', 'The essay highlights the dishabille of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dishearten', 'To cause someone to lose confidence', 'Critics argued that the plan might dishearten the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dishevel', 'Untidy, disordered (of clothes, appearance)', 'Scholars noted a striking dishevel between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dishonest', 'Not honest', 'What seemed dishonest at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disillusion', 'To free or deprive someone of idealism, belief, etc', 'The author hoped the new policy would disillusion tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disinclination', 'Reluctance, unwillingness to do something', 'Over time, the community learned to disinclination its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disinfect', 'To sterilize by the use of cleaning agent such as bleach', 'Over time, the community learned to disinfect its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disinfectant', 'A chemical agent used to destroy harmful organisms', 'Over time, the community learned to disinfectant its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disinformation', 'Publicly announced false information about a country', 'The essay highlights the disinformation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disingenuous', 'Not candid or sincere', 'What seemed disingenuous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disinherit', 'To exclude from inheritance, to disown', 'Critics argued that the plan might disinherit the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disinter', 'To dig up (something buries)', 'The author hoped the new policy would disinter tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disinterest', 'Having no stake or interest in the outcome, free of bias', 'What seemed disinterest at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disjunctive', 'Not connected, separated', 'What seemed disjunctive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dislocate', 'To put something out of its usual place', 'Critics argued that the plan might dislocate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dislodge', 'To remove or force out from a position', '“The earthquake dislodged several rocks from the cliff.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dismal', 'Disappointingly inadequate', 'Her dismal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dismissal', 'The act of sending someone away', 'The essay highlights the dismissal of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dismount', 'To get off (something)', 'Critics argued that the plan might dismount the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disobedience', 'Refusal to obey', 'Critics argued that the plan might disobedience the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disobedient', 'Not obedient', 'What seemed disobedient at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disown', 'To refuse to own or to refuse to acknowledge one’s own', 'Over time, the community learned to disown its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disparage', 'To belittle or criticize', '“She disparaged his efforts despite his hard work.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disparate', 'Essentially different in kind', 'The reviewer described the tone as disparate, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disparity', 'The state of being unequal, difference', 'Her disparity became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dispassionate', 'Not showing, and not affected by emotion, bias, or prejudice', 'The reviewer described the tone as dispassionate, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dispatch', 'To send a shipment with promptness', 'Over time, the community learned to dispatch its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dispel', 'To make a doubt or feeling disappear', 'The author hoped the new policy would dispel tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dispensation', 'The act of dispensing or dealing out, distribution', 'Her dispensation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dispers', 'Scattered or spread over a wide area', 'Her dispers became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disperse', 'To spread out in different directions', 'The author hoped the new policy would disperse tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Displace', 'To move something/someone, especially forcibly from a home', 'Over time, the community learned to displace its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disposition', 'To remove or place in a different position', 'Over time, the community learned to disposition its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dispossess', 'To deprive someone of the possession of land', 'The author hoped the new policy would dispossess tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disputation', 'Debate or argument, formal academic debate', 'The essay highlights the disputation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disputatious', '(of a person) fond of having heated arguments', 'The passage presents a disputatious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dispute', 'A disagreement, argument, or controversy', 'The essay highlights the dispute of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disqualify', 'To deprive of qualification or fitness', 'The author hoped the new policy would disqualify tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disquiet', 'Make (someone) worried or anxious', 'The essay highlights the disquiet of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disquietude', 'A state of uneasiness or anxiety', 'Her disquietude became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
